title: Centronia eximia
taxonomy: Centronia eximia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This family is commonly known as the melastome family and consists of about 175 genera and 5,000 species. The plant is native to the Americas and is part of the order Myrtales. The genus Centronia is a small group of plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Within the family Melastomataceae, Centronia eximia is a distinct species with its own unique characteristics.

intro: Centronia eximia is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the "Peruvian mint" due to its mint-like fragrance and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
